key_insight: In neutron-irradiated tungsten, strain-rate sensitivity and deformation length scale can be separated: strain rate mainly raises the flow stress, while indentation depth mainly governs plastic-zone size, pile-up, and GND buildup.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable constitutive models for irradiation-hardened metals under transient loading, improving prediction of indentation, contact, and compression responses across scales.
method: AFM and HR-EBSD to measure pile-up, residual strain, and GNDs
Strain-gradient CPFE with thermally activated slip, GND hardening, irradiation obstacles, and softening
evidence_signal: A single calibrated CPFE condition successfully validated against all other strain rates without retuning, and then reproduced independent variation of strain rate and indentation depth as well as polycrystal compression behavior.
watch_item: The model is calibrated on one experimental condition, so transfer to other irradiation states, temperatures, or loading paths should be checked before broad deployment.
paper_title: Decoupling Strain-Rate Sensitivity and Deformation Length Scale Effects in Neutron-Irradiated Tungsten: A Coupled Nano-Indentation, HR-EBSD and Crystal Plasticity Study
